title: harpalyce riparia
intro: Harpalyce riparia, also known as sticktight or hairy harpalyce, is a species of flowering plant in the legume family Fabaceae. It is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America and has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, primarily as a treatment for various skin conditions and wounds. The plant's roots and leaves contain compounds with anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which can help to reduce inflammation, prevent infection, and promote healing. Harpalyce riparia can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or topical ointment, making it a versatile herbal remedy for a range of health concerns.
